parentQuestion: Sound is generally perceived in the occipital lobe of the cerebral cortex.

scaffolds.q: Different brain lobes handle different sensory modalities.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Functional localization - each sense has dedicated cortical areas.
Correct! Primary visual cortex (V1) is in the occipital lobe.
Yes! Primary auditory cortex (A1) is in the temporal lobe.
Correct! Primary somatosensory cortex (S1) is in the postcentral gyrus.

scaffolds.q: Sound = hearing = temporal lobe, NOT occipital lobe.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Occipital is for vision. Don't confuse them!

scaffolds.q: Occipital damage → visual problems. Temporal damage → hearing/language problems.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Each lobe has specific functions.

scaffolds.q: The statement 'sound is perceived in occipital lobe' is INCORRECT.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Sound is temporal, not occipital.

scaffolds.q: Sound perception occurs in TEMPORAL cortex, not occipital.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Occipital = vision. Temporal = hearing. Know your lobes!

scaffolds.q: The answer is FALSE - sound is perceived in TEMPORAL, not occipital cortex.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! The question contains a common error. Vision = occipital. Hearing = temporal. The statement is false.
